# Overview

% start-badges

```
.. list-table::
    :stub-columns: 1

    * - docs
      - |docs|
    * - tests
      - | |coveralls| |codecov|
    * - package
      - | |version| |wheel| |supported-versions| |supported-implementations|
        | |commits-since|
```

% end-badges

JWT implementation for ZSL framework. This modules adds security
possibilities to ZSL.

- Free software: BSD license

## Installation

Just add `zsl_jwt` to your requirements or use

```
pip install zsl-jwt
```

## Usage

Add `zsl_jwt.module.JWTModule` to the modules in your `IoCContainer`
and provide a `zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTConfiguration` in your
configuration under `JWT` variable.

## Documentation

See more in <https://zsl-jwt.readthedocs.io/>

## Development

To run the all tests run:

```
tox
```

Note, to combine the coverage data from all the tox environments run:

```
.. list-table::
    :widths: 10 90
    :stub-columns: 1

    - - Windows
      - ::

            set PYTEST_ADDOPTS=--cov-append
            tox

    - - Other
      - ::

            PYTEST_ADDOPTS=--cov-append tox
```

modules.name: zsl_jwt.configuration
documentation: # {mod}`zsl_jwt.configuration`

classes.name: zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTConfiguration
documentation:
The main JWT configuration object. Consists of several token profiles
used for encoding/decoding. See {class}`zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile`.

classes.name: zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile

methods.name: zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile.secret
documentation: Key/secret used for encryption.

methods.name: zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile.issuer
documentation: The issuer name. This is a standard JWT claim.

methods.name: zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile.audience
documentation:
The audience of the token for which the token is intended.
This must match the audience used for decoding. This is a standard
JWT claim.

methods.name: zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile.expiration
documentation:
The time interval specifying when the token becomes invalid.
The token is valid until time now + expiration.
This is a standard JWT claim.

methods.name: zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile.not_before
documentation:
The time interval specifying when the token becomes valid.
The token is valid from time now + not_before.
This is a standard JWT claim.

Each profile consits of its own
 - secret and all the JWT claims definitions:
 - audience: `str` - must match the audience in the decoding,
 - issuer name: `str` - just an information about the token issuer,
 - expiration: `datetime.timedelta` specifying when token becomes invalid,
 - not_before: `datetime.timedelta` specifying when token becomes valid,
 - algorithm: encryption algorithm used to create the token.

documentation: # {mod}`zsl_jwt.module`

methods.name: zsl_jwt.module.JWTModule.provide_jwt_configuration
documentation:
Returns the JWT configuration.

```
param config

Injected. Configuration object

return

Current JWT configuration.
```

JWT module activation. Add to your IoCContainer to enable JWT support.

It just provides the {class}`zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTConfiguration` so
that the users may read the JWT configuration.

modules.name: zsl_jwt.auth.controller
documentation:
# {mod}`zsl_jwt.controller`

Contains the login function.

functions.name: zsl_jwt.auth.controller.authenticate
documentation:
The "login" function, from the given username/password returns the JWT.
Use only as a delegate function in handling of tasks.

```
param username



param password



param authentication_service

Injected. Uses
 to query
the database.

return

error response if the credentials are invalid or jwt.
```

modules.name: zsl_jwt.codec
documentation:
# {mod}`zsl_jwt.codec`

The module provides the two main functions {func}`.encode` and {func}`.decode`
which encode and decode the given payload.

functions.name: zsl_jwt.codec.encode
documentation:
Encodes the payload.
\:param payload: The payload to be encoded.
\:param jwt_configuration: The JWT configuration, it is injected.
\:param algorithm: Algorithm name. A reasonable default is provided.
\:param profile: The JWT profile to be used. See

> {class}`zsl_jwt.configuration.JWTProfile`.

```
return

The JWT token.
```

functions.name: zsl_jwt.codec.decode
documentation:
Decodes the encoded token.

```
param token

The encoded token.

param jwt_configuration

The configuration, injected.

param profile

The profile name used for encoding.

return

The decoded payload.
```

classes.name: zsl_jwt.codec.ZslJwtError
documentation: Main exception type raised from this module.

classes.name: zsl_jwt.codec.ZslJwtInvalidAudienceError
documentation:
When the audience of the token does not match the
audience of the profile used to decode the token.

modules.name: zsl_jwt.auth.service
documentation:
# {mod}`zsl_jwt.auth.service`

The abstraction of authentication service and user information.

functions.name: zsl_jwt.auth.service.create_standard_user_information
documentation: Creates the user information/representation from the given parameters.

functions.name: zsl_jwt.auth.service.decode_to_standard_user_information
documentation: Creates the user information/representation from the given auth token.

classes.name: zsl_jwt.auth.service.AuthenticationService

methods.name: zsl_jwt.auth.service.AuthenticationService.verify_password
documentation:
Verifies if the username, password combination is valid.
Returns true iff it is, False otherwise. It should not raise
exceptions.

methods.name: zsl_jwt.auth.service.AuthenticationService.get_user_information
documentation:
Returns the user information for the given username.
\:param username:
\:return: Tuple with the first element being the list of roles

> (list of strings) and a user information, may be `None`.

The service used for verifying username and password and
querying the user information.

classes.name: zsl_jwt.auth.service.StandardUserInformation
documentation:
Standard user information - contains username,
roles (list of strings) and a user object, if wanted (may be `None`)

The configuration of the authentication.

documentation:
The string identifying the class implementing the
{class}`zsl_jwt.auth.service.AuthenticationService` which is used

Auth module configuration. It holds the string identifying the

modules.name: zsl_jwt

modules.name: zsl_jwt.auth.module
documentation:
# {mod}`zsl_jwt.auth.module`

This ZSL module reads the auth configuration and provides
{class}`zsl_jwt.auth.configuration.AuthConfiguration` and an instance of
{class}`zsl_jwt.auth.service.AuthenticationService`.

methods.name: zsl_jwt.auth.module.AuthModule.provide_jwt_configuration
documentation:

```
param config

Injected. Configuration object

return

Current authentication/authorization configuration.
```

methods.name: zsl_jwt.auth.module.AuthModule.provide_authentication_service

modules.name: zsl_jwt.decorators
documentation:
# {mod}`zsl_jwt.decorators`

The module provides a decorator which turns the function's output into a JWT.

functions.name: zsl_jwt.decorators.jwt_output
documentation:
Uses the returned dictionary as the JWT payload and returns
a JSON with the token.

```
param profile

The JWT profile which is used.

param debug

The debug info - if true or the debug mode is on,
then the payload is in the returned JSON.

return

Dict with the 'token' key containing the created JWT.
```
